title: [Feature]: Add subject-aware observability certification tests and runbook
problem: Subject-aware observability can leak sensitive routing structure and can create high-cardinality metric sets. A production feature needs certification tests and operator guidance before it can be enabled safely.
proposal: Add a focused certification suite and runbook for subject-aware observability. The suite should prove disabled-by-default behavior, allow/deny handling, cardinality caps, overflow behavior, sanitized output, malformed policy rejection, connector parity, and delivery non-interference.
users: Release engineers, security reviewers, operators approving subject-family dashboards, and maintainers validating observability connector behavior.
delivery_semantics: Certification must prove that subject-aware observability does not alter ACK behavior, sink writes, retry handling, or DLQ publication when export is disabled, denied, capped, stale, malformed, or failing.
security: Test fixtures and runbook examples must use fake subjects and must not include real server addresses, credentials, sensitive operational subjects, classified values, payloads, customer names, file paths, or table names.
acceptance: Tests prove subject-aware export is disabled by default.
Tests prove allow, deny, malformed policy, and cardinality cap behavior.
Tests prove output contains approved family labels but not raw subjects by default.
Tests prove observability failures do not change delivery decisions.
Runbook explains when not to enable subject-aware export.
tests: Add unit tests, connector tests, and failure-injection tests for the subject-aware policy and aggregation layers. Add optional integration tests only behind explicit markers. Run scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update subject-aware observability evaluation, observability, Prometheus, testing, operations, security, roadmap, and changelog documentation.
closeout: Close after the release containing subject-aware observability certification tests and runbook guidance is published. Include release tag, test evidence, documentation, and limitations.
